original_abstract: An iron-catalyzed protocol has been developed for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes. This methodology exhibits a broad substrate scope and excellent tolerance for diverse functional groups, enabling the efficient incorporation of various perfluoroalkyl sources, including the challenging gaseous reagent CF3I. The resulting perfluoroalkylated alkyl and alkenyl iodides serve as versatile intermediates that can be further elaborated through various cross-coupling transformations. By utilizing earth-abundant iron catalysis, this approach offers a straightforward, streamlined, and sustainable route to sophisticated perfluoroalkylated organic molecules.
humanized_abstract: An iron-catalyzed procedure for the intermolecular 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes has been established. A wide variety of substrates and functionalities are compatible with this protocol. Common perfluoroalkyl chain sources that include the air sensitive gaseous reagent CF3I can be employed. The produced perfluoroalkylated alkyl and alkenyl iodides are useful precursors for a variety of cross-coupling reactions. Utilizing the low-cost iron as catalyst, the new procedure enables efficient synthesis of complex perfluoroalkylated organic molecules in an efficient, simple and eco-friendly way.
